review_text: Stunning location on the Tumut River. Friendly and helpful staff who went above and beyond to assist me. Families, older people and solos are well catered for, whether it's a tent, caravan or cabin experience you are looking for.

The lady on reception was just lovely. It's so nice to be greeted with a smile after a long drive.  We stayed in a powered caravan site, with our dog. Lovely big site, lovely green grass, lovely clean amenities. The park is right on the Tumut River, and a short drive into town for the shops. Highly recommended. Five stars.

We’d definitely recommend this caravan park! The staff were so friendly and welcoming, and we loved our unpowered camping site — right beside the fast-flowing Tumut River where the sound of rushing water was pure bliss. Fires are allowed, which made for a cozy evening under the stars.  The amenities were spotless, with great water pressure and lovely hot showers. Such a peaceful and relaxing spot all round!
Reflections Tumut should upgrade to a key pad code entry to the toilet amenities. It’s  frustrating to have only 1 key issued for the amenities block use (my only issue with our stay).

Great place to stay. Views of the river are awesome. Staff Zoe and Hanna couldn't do enough to make us feel welcome and went above and beyond to assist on every occasion.  One of my favourite places. Highly recommended 👌
everything was terrible  and not good I've gone through the worst experience of my life in this place

This is supposed to be the superior cabin. I don’t think so. We stayed in a superior cabin in the Discovery Park in Kinka beach QLD. It was as old as this one. But much bigger, with a car awning. And no cockatoo’s. We came here at a good time, end of February 2026. The park was only a third full. Very peaceful and very quiet of a night. We’ve driven down from Bingara. Straight south to Gunnedah Mudgee Blayney Cowra and now Tumut. The country on this drive has been terribly dry. And is desperately in need of a good soaking. The locals have told us that once houses were covered with dam water, are now reamerging with the very low water levels at Blowering dam. The locals are concerned. We were supposed to get a storm on the 22/2/26. All we had was a bit of a drizzle all day. There’s a lot of cabins here. It must get pretty packed on school holidays. I was sleeping in the second bedroom. I was a bit misdified, when they handed me at the office, my sheets, pillows and blanket. To take to our cabin. I can’t believe how lazy they are here. You’d think, they’d have them in the cabin. After all, we did book over the phone. To weeks prior before arriving. I’ve not had this happen at other Reflections. Or any where else for that matter. On

Stayed here a few weeks ago , Great caravan park, the girls in the office are great to deal with and Scott the groundsman was very helpful with his local knowledge, he’s a great asset to this caravan park.
The manager though seems he would be best suited to another role as he’s not a people person by any means.I recommend this park to anyone who likes fishing and walking.

Gorgeous park - we had a riverfront site which was absolutely perfect, you dont even need to leave the park. There are adjoining walk/bike paths to the surrounding parks and wetlands. Walk to town. Will definitely be back - loved this place and so did my 2 teenage kids.
Great for work or family holidays facilities have all recently been upgraded, was a very comfortable stat

Beautiful location to attend the falling leaf festival. Easy walk to most festivities.  Great hosts. Beautiful grounds. Would have been nice to have a dog off leash area. Didn't like key system for bathrooms as I often needed to use them when I didn't have key with me!

Had a really good stay in Reflections Tumut River - Holiday Park

Booked an unpowered site next to the river and had a lovely view. In the morning the fog in the river is quite magnificent.

The site is spacious and quiet and there are walking tracks nearby. There are also wildlife nearby (ducks, magpies, kookaburra, cockatoos, possums, and horses across the river)

The amenities are spacious and clean.

I had a really nice time and will surely be back ❤️

relaxing stay on the river edge, a short stroll to the wetlands walk|great pictures of autumn leaves and reflections in the water|family friendly during the day (kids playing and on bikes) but quiet at might|coffee van utilised in the mornings|this was our second visit which we booked for longer than our first |we'll be back
Great quiet site, close (easy walk) along the river or into town for facilities and supplies.

We stayed at Reflections Tumut River last Easter and had such a great experience that we rebooked nearly a year in advance, hoping to make it a regular tradition. Unfortunately, after our recent dealings with reception and the site manager, we will definitely not be returning.
We had three families planning to travel (a 7-hour trip), but due to the fuel shortage and rising prices at the time, we contacted the park to ask if there was any flexibility to reschedule, receive a credit, or a refund. Given the circumstances—and public advice to avoid unnecessary fuel use—we felt this was a reasonable request. However, there was no real willingness to assist. The only option offered was a $25 fuel contribution and 10% off a future stay if we returned by May 2026, which we did not feel was a fair or practical solution given the situation.
We even found another family, who lived much closer, willing to take over our booking. Despite this, we were told this was not allowed, and the response we received suggested the issue was more about not receiving additional payment rather than helping customers find a solution.
To make matters worse, we later received an email stating that our bookings had been cancelled—despite us clearly saying we were still trying to attend. The site manager claimed that we had asked for the cancellation, which is simply not true. During our phone conversation, he was extremely rude and repeatedly cut me off by saying “no, no, no,” which was both frustrating and highly unprofessional.
As returning customers, this was incredibly disappointing. We had every intention of continuing to holiday here each year, but based on this experience, we will not be coming back.

Beautiful caravan park within walking distance of the Tumut town centre. |Friendly staff. We stayed in a Tiny House. Ours was right on the river and so peaceful and picturesque. Friendly park with lots of travellers all up for a chat.
Awesome spot. Rooms vary, but camp sites are amazing. The river close and a few nearby walks that are lovely. Walk to town if you like. Love this place.
It is a beautiful camping ground and reception staff were fantastic
The best setting for a caravan park, and great service and friendly staff.
Easy walk back to town. Great location on the river.

Great wee caravan park, some sites a bit squeezed, but requested change and got larger site with no issues.  Water connection not available at every site unless hose extensions and multi tap connections available 🤗
Lovely campsite on the Tumut River.  Sites spacious.  Camp kitchen clean. Bathrooms  clean.

Superb site by the river.
Water was just right when we visited.
We had a fabulous stay. Everything was neat and tidy and well maintained. We will definitely stay again.
Fantastic location and beautifully maintained campground. Couldn’t fault it.

We loved the park and surrounds .The booking and processes of instructions for getting into the park and access to the cabin was easy |The Cabin however,was very outdated and not as clean as expected. Grubby marks on door and dust on outside table . There was a non-generous supply of milk|The price of $160 a night was overpriced for the facility.
Highly recommended this beautiful camp site. Right on the river with an added bonus of a friendly platapus
Great park, playground for kids, river was refreshing to swim in
Tiny homes are new cosy and located beside the beautiful Tumut river. Highly recommended for a couple for a short stay.

Big sites, shady and cool- great parks and open spaces- good camp kitchen.

Swimming hole and grass area is fantastic, if you are crap at swimming or have small kids, wear a life jacket as the river flows like fast.

The old toilet amenities need a bit of a refurb and the cleaners need a change over- they are cleaned daily, but it’s a bit of a once over if that-  there are wall boogers in the cubicles that are older than the current fossil records.- it’s definently a thong wearing shower experience and burn anything that touches the floors.

Overall- pretty good park- I have stayed before and will definitely stay again.
Had a fantastic time at Refections. Beautiful park. Lovely trees and the river is spectacular. Great location. Happy to recommend.

Reflections Tumut is a truly perfect campground. The entire site is fully shaded, making it exceptionally pleasant even on warmer days. The camping areas are flat, well-leveled, and set on beautiful grassy lawns, which adds greatly to the overall comfort. The facilities and amenities are clean, well maintained, and very comfortable.

There are long, flat cycling paths suitable for families as well as solo riders, which we thoroughly enjoyed. The campground is only about one kilometer from the town center, which offers everything you could want: great cafés, restaurants, pubs, and lovely shops for strolling and exploring.

We stayed here for five days and would happily have stayed longer, if we weren’t continuing our wonderful journey across the amazing continent Australia.
An outstanding campground and a memorable stop on our travels.
Thank you 🙏

Good park, good location. It was really hot when we were there, park was full and the amenities were always dirty, which let the whole experience down. At peak time when the park is full they need another cleaning shift.
Lovely campsite.  Close to pool,  shops and library.  River is COLD! Lots of Shade and amenities are lovely.

We stayed for 3 nights. Unfortunately, our campsite was close to the road, so there was some car noise. In the mornings, the birds were very active. Additionally, the staff member, Brayn, was really rude, which was disappointing.

We stayed there for 3 nights at powerd campsite
And the guy his name was Bryan, he was very rude specially to people from background.
And very noisy place in the morning by wild birds
Great powered caravan site on the river

Had an overall good experience over the NY break, Unfortunately our site was in terrible condition due to recent installation of tiny homes along the river. (Site 24) Site 23 also needs fixed asap. Site 23 couldn't park where they should have which then put us out of wack on our site moving us away from our water & electrical connection. Had to go buy another extension cable & couldnt connect to water. This was a bit of a pain given wed paid for the conference & where staying 1 week. ||Overall the rest of the park is great & shower blocks where well looked after given it was so busy. 8kph speed limits where being followed by cars/Ute's but our son was nearly bowled over a few times with kids on e bikes & scooters. Kids will be kids! ||Great park but avoid site 23/24 up the side beside the walking track until its fixed correctly.
No toilet or shower in the cabin.

Overall we had a nice stay, they park was quite busy but quiet at the same time.
Where we were allocated appeared to be river front however that was quite deceptive as there were many trees blocking the river and no grass by our caravan. Other areas of the park were wonderfully grassy, aside from this it was a nice park.

Booked cabin 19 for 3 nights, could tell from website photos it was quite dated but felt that the location right next to the river would make up for that. We arrived tired after a long drive and were very disappointed with the cabin, not only outdated but run down and dirty, the whole place was in need of a deep clean. The shower sills were grubby, skirtings all dirty, light fittings covered in dust, window sills with dead insects. On the deck outside, the area next to the BBQ was black with grease which you could smell, so even our enjoyment of sitting outside was spoilt. It was quire obvious the place hadn't been cleaned properly for a long time.|We left after 2 nights and the manager did refund the 3rd night, would have left after 1 night but were too tired to face the long drive to Melbourne. With hindsight I should have asked for a full refund as we didn't get what we paid for.
Absolutely lovely park, lots of trees for shade, we were lucky enough to get a riverside site and it was great. Very friendly check in and informative. Amenities clean and handy.

Park Beutiful Park and Locations, N/a review for rooms, on Caravan site. This Review is for Constructive use only for Park Management and fellow travellers. 1. Dump Point only one and was informed that only one is permitted in Tourist parks, BS as permission should be sought from Local Council. 2. Cleanliness of Toilet and Showers are not being kept up to hygiene and general tidiness standards and staff are usually around the back smoking or Talking. 3. Trash and Recycling bins only for Cabins, when borrowed the grumpy staff confiscate them day after with hostile remarks. So think as a ex manager of a park, please get your acts together.
The park itself is a nice park ,the river front sites are very nice lacking   some grass to keep the dust down

A beautiful riverside location although a long walk into the town.

Let down by dirty bathrooms in the camping area

Staff are friendly
Such a pretty park .. manager was very kind and helpful . Would definitely stay again . 👍🏻
Always a pleasure. Great sites and location. Sites great sizes. Staff always helpful. Amenities clean. New owners (Reflections) have made lots of great improvements.

We unfortunately only had an overnight stay but what a beautiful park. We arrived late but everything was waiting for us. Best site on the edge of the river. Friendly and respectful occupants. My only regret was that we couldn’t stay longer. Amenities were spotless. Thank you
Such a lovely and well.run caravan park
A great cabin.  Very comfortable.  Share amenities was very relaxed.  Staff were very nice and friendly on arriving and leaving.  Highly recommend.
One of the prettiest caravan parks’  I’ve been to in my 4 years travelling. The grounds are immaculate.  Camped on the river edge 💕 The staff were always smiling and helpful. Great job 👏
Duck

We are returning visitors to the park. There is enough space for everyone, the amenities are clean. After being taken over by Reflections, new cabins and kitchen upgrades are underway. We appreciated the text updates and severe weather alert that we received during the stay.
Was amazing we did go down and hopes in was amazing filling vibing with mother nature.🩷💞🥰🏞️
It was fine

We had a powered site for our caravan,  and the view from the door of our van was absolutely amazing. Lovely flowing river > lush green grass. A great park in a beautiful location with awesome scenery 👍👍👍
Carefully maintained gardens and very clean amenities.

Just loved our stay over the long weekend.  Big family get together.  Scott really helped with some extra bins and checked daily if there was anything we needed.  Zoe was also very helpful in the office. We will return soon.
Not the best when it rains
Love it here, quiet, friendly. A great place to stay.

I want to give a big shout out to Scott.
He was amazing. Nothing is too much trouble. He lifted stuff in to the cabin, as my partner isn't well. He called around most days to see if there was anything we needed. And he came in on his day off to lift stuff back into the car.
I can't thank him enough

Booked as we thought it would be convenient to Mt Selwyn snow fields. Much better options in the other direction amd certainly wouldn't recommend it. Over an hour away and dangerously bendy/windy downhill run into Tumut.
Booked for 2 adults & 2 kids. The receptionist seemed overly frustrated that we needed linen for 3 beds as she'd only prepared for 2? Due to being understaffed and likely overworked, I think common sense went out the window as it seems painfully obvious we'd need 3 sets! Anyways, after a long drive, arrived to find linen for 3 beds on the dining table. Not sure if this is normal for all booked cabins to make their own beds or if she just couldn't be bothered. As a family of 4 we definitely struggled to find anywhere to hang our snow gear to dry of an evening. Doubt we'll be welcomed back after this review but not planning to ever return. As for the positives. Gorgeous view of the river and some friendly caravan folk staying in the park. Also very close to town which has more pubs then any town would realistically require...but go Tumut!
A favourite park of ours. Tumut is well worth a village. A short hours drive min the winter and you should see snow! The village has everything you need. Including its own brewery. Beeeeerrr. Mmmmm

We absolutely love coming back to Tumut and staying at Reflections caravan park.
Scott the caretaker/ everything man is fantastic, you will not meet a nicer person.
Some people staying at the park used the washing machines and left them in a dirty mess. When I reported it to Zoe in the office she was so apologetic and got straight onto it.
Scott come around to let us know he has cleaned them out and apologised on the behalf off the grubs that left them dirty.

The park is close to town and the local Brewery.
We will be back
The Blaney’s

What a great friendly caravan park for short or long stay. Only cane to Tumut for 1 night on way up North and rang to see if needed to book site.. told not to worry would be able to pick own site on arrival. Was met by extremely friendly staff on arrival and allocated “best site in the park” no 1 … with beautiful views of river but was actually the best site for us as it was so large we didn’t have to unhitch! In fact most sites have great views. Had a beautiful fire by the river with wood delivered by a local service. Amenities very clean and serviceable and good camp kitchen. Would have love to stay longer… will be back very efficient team
Stunning place on the river. Staff knowledgeable and friendly
Great place to stay right on the beautiful river

Love this caravan park on a beautiful walk by the river. Clean and tidy facilities
Cabin wasn’t brand new and tea was in short supply, but comfortable!

Have stayed at Tumut many times. But since the new management took over the park it always seems short for staff.
The toilets were unfortunately very messy, bad smelling and no toilet paper this past weekend during cider festival. Many people did complain but nothing was done to help out.. hopefully the new management can address these issues and bring the park back to its former glory

We love this caravan park. Everything is great and well maintained.
We met the grounds keeper Scott and he was very lovely man and made sure everyone is happy.
We extended our stay and we will come back as well in few weeks time.

Unreasonable Charges for Infants: They are charging 1.5-year-old child as an adult, which is highly unusual and feels like a rip-off. I’ve camped in many places and have never come across a campsite that counts a toddler as a full-paying guest.

Booking Inconvenience: We were forced to book a second campsite just for one friend because they counted our infant as a full person— This policy seems unfair and illogical.

No Option to Choose Campsite: There is no feature on the website to select specific spots during booking. We booked two sites on different dates and requested they be placed side by side due to having a child, but the staff made no effort to accommodate this at check-in.

Unhelpful Staff: The check-in staff were indifferent and dismissive. No effort to put the two sites together even though the next day, most of the sites remained empty.  Despite explaining that we had a child and requesting a corner spot, they refused to make any adjustments or even show concern.

No Fire Pits Provided: Surprisingly, campsites do not come with built-in fire pits. You’re expected to bring your own. For the price being charged, this is unacceptable and not in line with most other campgrounds.

Washroom Key Not Provided: At check-in, I was not given a key to the washrooms. I only realised this later when I found the facilities locked. Had I not returned before 5 PM, I would have had no access to a toilet that night.
Great place. On the river. Good bathrooms. Very clean. Walk to lots of places.

Had 2 nights camping on the riverbank thanks to Chloe 💐 who found us the perfect location.
Amenities were clean and well stocked with handsoap and loo roll!
Team were friendly.
The 4 Chicks coffee van is a little gem ♡♡♡♡.
Wonderful clean campground.
Wish we had taken our kayaks!!
Nosey possums were a delight to get up close for photos!
We will return. This place is AWESOME!
A beautiful park. Clean amenities and helpful staff

This was a great place to stay but has recently come under the management of the Reflections Group. They are severely overpriced and their management at the corporate level is completely useless. Our stay saw regular site double bookings stuff ups. Unfortunately this facility is now overpriced and likely to get dearer. Reflections have taken over and wrecked other locations as well. They are managed by a govt appointed "Boys Club".

Top price for a second grade park. The amenities should be cleaned at least twice a day with this many guests staying.  The spider webs need a dam good clean.  Camp kitchen should be cleaned properly ,not just a quick wipe over.

Went with some friends to the music festival. Enjoyed it. Lots of space. Stayed on a powered site and pitched a tent. Shower and toilet facilities are very clean and well kept. Constant hot water and no timer in sight. Staff were friendly and the small pop up caravan cafe there made a great coffee and one of the owners there was very pleasant and chatty. (In a good way,)
A great location on the river but there isn’t much else positive to say. Amenities and the park itself were poorly maintained. E
Great park,showers and toilets Immaculate

Nice spot, positives: near river, great for kids with big Play area.
Negative: amenities not cleaned over weekend and plane trees leaves everywhere.

The previous people that ran the place were unreal. I lost my son to suicide while I was there and the previous staff were so supportive. The new folks won't  let me stay there now. Not sure why. The grounds keeper there was really good to me back in 2019 so I gave him $1000.00 bucks just to do something good for him. When I couldn't  stay  there anymore he blocked me on messages. I used to love that place but not anymore.
Great place to sit and watch the river go by. Even caught  fish.

A beautiful caravan park location on the river. Lots of nicely grassed and shaded campsites to the area left of the office. To the right the spots have a lot more dirt and less grass. Yes there is a bit of truck noise at times but meh its not that bad at all and it quietens down at night as expected. Either way I would suggest avoiding the sites up near the road.
There are lots!! of trees and one review commented on low branches on roadways, yes there are some and yes management could prune just the branches that hang a bit low on the tracks but they are also fairly easy to dodge around where you need to as you drive through the park.
Weekends and holidays you will probably get some bigger groups all camping together. We had a few but they were no problem and generally considerate at night with their noise. Watch out if parking under one of the Black Walnut trees, they have a hard fruit that looks a bit like a lime, you may end up with some bombing on your roof as they randomly fall, I saw some parrots biting them off.
The amenities were maintained and cleaned well, no issues there at all.
There was a lack of any obvious grounds maintenance activity whilst we stayed. This was good in some ways as it made for a tranquil atmosphere. The grass had been cut but there is an enormous amount of leaf litter build up particularly in a few spots and accumulating on paths.
The river front sites were nice and hot property. Due to the amount of occupancy they get and shading these have a lot of dirt and less grass than other camp sites in the park. Book ahead if you are planning on stopping over a weekend and want a river front site, be clear about your van size as some were small, but there are heaps of larger sites that are not river front. The ones that backed onto the Western fence line were nice particularly the ones back from the road. Mid week outside of holidays I expect a few river front sites would be available at short notice.
Great spot, fantastic clear river. Park amenities clean,  walking distance to the pub. Plenty of room. Would come back again.

Disgusting service by the manager woman pulled in asked for river front no trees .
Went to site trees touching either side and all trees very low 1m lower than rooftop of van that's not on.. She called then said your site is booked out go have a look at these others, so whilst trying to drive around dodging many low branches with a 37ft van we were not happy with sites under trees everywhere and her complete Rudeness so money refunded and out we went...only if desperate you want river site but apparently all are booked out barely a van there.
Ok spot. A lot of road noise weekdays at night3am onwards trucks (weekend was ok) clean ammenities
Nice location for camping.
Booked 2 nights for power site. Plenty of trees, shady spot. Clean bathroom n toilets.
Great place to relax by the river. Large grassed area for kids to play.
Super friendly guidance when booking. Large sites, with many toilets and showers kept very clean. Nicely kept grass next to the water. Lovely place, many thanks.
Well maintained c/van park right on the beach, kid friendly, great place 👍

Fantastic park, location on the river excellent. We had an onsuite site, was very clean and modern. All grass sites and lovely trees.
There is a great walking track out of the park.

Just looked at your website - photos of ceilings, rivers and footy grounds are not helpful!!!

Yes, have a look at standard cabin 5 berth.
I’m looking on an iPad - maybe that is the problem??

friendly staff and pleasant check in experience.... shady Riverside camping.... busy but not roudy... Tumit is a pleasant high country town... Be sure to drop into Drifter Camping (opposite the pool) for a coffee while in town.

Basic amenities should include stable WiFi, just like hot water or AC. Unfortunately, the hotel's poor internet infrastructure made basic tasks frustrating. In today's connected world, this is a major service failure that affects guest satisfaction and safety.

A relaxing holiday shouldn't mean complete digital isolation unless by choice. The hotel needs to understand that good WiFi isn't a luxury anymore - it's a basic necessity for modern travelers.
We camped here again recently. Ended up staying 2 nights. Didn’t start the car the second day.  Lose to town. Magic. Love this place.

Riverglad Caravan Park (CP) is a delighful, spacious location which offers river views, is within distance of town has an array of birdlife and satisftying ammentities.
The option for a fire is avialable, but BYO Pit and wood.
Staff were friendly, helpful and contactable.
Its a great base for exploring the wider area, as shops are close to restock with supplies or grab takeaway or a meal at the pub after a hard days adventure.
There is lots of shade from GIANT and lots of lots of space. Cover food at night as possums linger in those big trees.
I recommend sitting by the river, with a good meal, and listening to the birds.
The kingfishers do not dissapoint, waking up to a chorus of these little guys was superb!  Also spotted a pair of dollarbirds ( my first for the season).
Oh, you might have to share your space with some ducks who are all too happy to come say hello, if food is about. They are polite though, so its all good.
Everyone was good about the 10pm noise limit and it seems to be a clientel of grey nomads.
Great caravan park on the Tumut River.
Great location. Very pretty surroundings.

Great facilities, well maintained and close to town. The sound of engine braking semi trailers and cars goes all night …. If you’re in a caravan maybe OK but in a tent forget it you won’t sleep a wink.

We stayed at Roverglade for three nights on site 6 right in the banks of the Tumut river. Such a beautiful park with walk / bike ways into town and along the river. Great place to set up camp and explore the snowy mountains

Lovely quiet park on the banks of the Tumut River, short walk to town. Shady, grassy sites with power and water, some with river views. Older style amenities but clean, well equipped camp kitchen. Nice place to stop and relax.
I heard it is a great place to hangout with friends

We stayed here about 10 years ago and finally were able to return. Beautiful location on the edge of the Tumut River and within walking distance of town. Cabin was clean and well equipped. Reception staff friendly and helpful. Various walking tracks nearby.

Camped here 2 nights.
Super friendly staff.
Well set up park that is well looked after.
Amenties very clean.
River is beautiful.
Close wall to town/pubs.

We stayed here for a night and the camp sites and location were nice. We however for the price of a powered site for 1 night were extremely disappointed. The cleanliness of the facilities particularly for the men was so bad my partner refused to use them. The women’s whilst not as bad were still not great. Again, great location and could’ve been a great experience but was very let down by the toilet and shower facilities.

Stayed in a 6 berth standard cabin .Older style cabin all the usual inclusions linen, cooking facilities ,utensils Air con TV etc. Four bunks and a separate main bedroom .Double bed ,bonus electric blankets .Clean cabin older but clean bathroom. Nice location short walk to the Tumut river .Even threw in a few casts but the trout weren't biting. Well kept grounds . A resident coffee hut a very pleasant stay.
great riverside sites with clean toilets
Just beautiful,

A fantastic park for all your stress free holiday. Immaculate y clean amenities. Disabled access and toilets. Well maintained sites. All the facilities are provided for children's playground. Camp kitchen and large  TV. Mostly grass  sites a worry if excive  rain  but overall a terrific place Dennis
Absolutely ideallic spot right on the river with amazing facilities... spotless and modern.  Walking distance to town but who'd want to leave this camp spot!
Nice park in nice town, can be a little heavy on the rules and regs but can’t fault it.
Excellent location on the beautiful Tumut river.

Camped here, on the river, on a non powered site. The park is lovely with walks which branch out from the park along the river and to kids play areas and a meditation peace circle/ labyrinth, right by the pond.
The Falling Leaves festival is on from tomorrow to honour the beauty of the changing colours and falling leaves. Tumut has gorgeous coloured trees of all colours in Autumn.
The walk into town is about 10 mins only.
Amenities were clean n tidy but we didn’t need to use the showers ( had our own).

Absolutely terrible conditions, so probably not a proper review, Powered Site was very wet and muddy , Low hanging trees made it hard to put van on higher ground , Office staff where brilliant, Easy walking distance to town and brewery,
Beautiful location, nice relaxed caravan park. Plenty of room around sites, cabins.
Beautiful caravan park right on the Tumut River. Stayed in a 6 berth cabin which was huge! Very nicely fitted out, great location and facilities. Would definitely recommend and will return. 😁
Lovely location. River sites were all taken, but still ok
Called in late...service was great...was busy..but enjoyed stay.

Great spot to stay in Tumut, also a great venue for 'Rock the Turf' annual music festival already celebrating their 10th anniversary. Very talented local bands along with 19 Twenty and Deisle made for great entertainment! A very well organized event at a beautiful venue.
It's a great park right on the river. The big thing missing is a pool. The river is too dangerous for swimming.
As I have posted before this is one of the best caravan parks in southern  NSW not to be missed
Bit pricey for a very basic cabin.

A great van park. Easy walking distance to town and the brewery. Sites are generally large and well-grassed. A wise word though, over the Christmas/New Year period and a few weeks either side, a very large stand of London Plane trees will be dumping seeds. Additionally, the corellas and other birds will assist those seeds to fall. My tip, if you visit at that time, ask for a site away from the plane trees. Also, gas bottle refills are well-priced. All in all, a great spot from which to visit local towns and sites.
Cannot fault this caravan park. We stayed 3 days last year and ate back for 7 this year, maybe 10 next year. Amenities always clean, large mostly shady sites, great walks and easy access into Tumut

Right on the river. Short walk to the Brewery. Plenty of space and great facilities
Thanks for having us team

Awesome caravan park on the outskirts of town but within walking distance to everything needed, awesome spot on the river with massive sites and plenty of them, nice toilets get dirty quickly but cleaners in there every day, twice during peak period, river is very refreshing but fun for a plunge, no Bindis which was a dream barefoot no problems lots of parking even out the front friendly staff and well stocked front desk, drinks, ice, gas everything you need. Definitely coming back and would recommend anybody to stay here and explore the area.
I left some headphones here and they found them and sent them back free of charge! Great service and good location.
Great place to stay lots of room Great for the family  stayed in cabins
Great Park, plenty of shade, on the river

Just spent a few days in the Caravan.
Beautiful park, shady, clean, friendly wildlife, kookaburras.
My Only Gripe was being woken up just before midnight by the loud sound of banging bin lids, bottles.
A Male on a pushbike was Rummaging through the Bins.
I Felt a little unsafe.☹
He rode off as a few Caravan Lights came on!

My extended family and I have stayed here twice in the last two years.  The location is fantastic and the range of accommodation choices is very good. This is also one of the cleanest and tidiest places I have ever stayed.  We all enjoy our stays here immensely.  I would highly recommend.
Lovely spot and so nice to be right on the river
Wonderful location. Very pleasant.
Own van great park on the tumut river.  Lovely green grass sites lots of bird life great walks friendly service
Lovely clean, comfortable cabin! Beautiful river & green surroundings!

Very beautiful area.
Sites are good but they need marking out as some people tend to park rather close 🙄
Also drainage for grey water is blocked staff are aware stated just put it on the grass.
Amenities are very clean.
Overall would stay again.
Power and Water hook up is $42 per night
Great caravan park nice amenities clean and tidy
Wonderful place well worth a visit

This place was great. We stayed here for 4 days and the sound of the river was very calming. We couldn't fish sadly due to it not being the right season, so we just resorted to going to the nearby Blowering Dam. Some of the people are quite rude as on our last night we experienced racism from a couple of drunk men and they gave threats to one of my dogs as he was barking at them. Overall, 8/10
Absolutely beautiful spot!! Staff are extremely friendly and helpful. The property is immaculate and the facilities are clean and extremely user friendly
Very nice Caravan Park, but the Jayco Cabins are very old and as they are Jayco they are poor quality anyway.
We stayed In  the new family cabins with 3 bedrooms and 2 bathrooms. Very modern and clean. I definitely recommend these rooms as most other cabins were dated. Very nice location
We spent 5 days at the park for the hotrod show. Park was beautiful, location perfect, amenities spotless and staff wonderful ❤️

Cabin bedrooms very compact and caravan-like.
Not very sound-proof as I could hear the neighbors flush the toilet.
Location near the river and view of the river made up for the visit.

We were six families staying here in 5 cabins. My family stayed in a cabin that was so closer to the river. Nice stay here. We didn't cook here and stayed one night only.

Pros:
1. Beautiful river view from the cabin
2. Clean cabin
3. Nice stay here
4. Good spa and clean bathroom

Cons:
1. Main bed wasn't comfortable but manageable
2. Bathroom exhaust fan wasn't working

We will stay again soon.

I stay in alot of caravan parks but must say this one is exceptional! From when you check in to check out, staff are super friendly and the grounds and cabins are super clean. The scenery and location are stunning!

Great place to stay in tumut. We mucked up our late arrival and were still helped and given a key to our cabin even though it was our fault. The cabin was super nice and very cozy. Everything that we needed, and the view of the river with the mist coming off it was beautiful. Super happy. Would definitely come back!
